concise.name: Concise
concise.desc: Direct and clear tone — good for daily or minor updates

structured.name: Structured
structured.desc: Categorized and scannable tone — good for large releases with multiple changes

benefitLed.name: Benefit-led
benefitLed.desc: Value-focused tone — good for major features you want to highlight

witty.name: Witty
witty.desc: Playful and light tone — good for routine maintenance or bug fixes

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.subtitle: All submissions
feedback.searchNoMatchHint: Try a different term, or clear the search.

sso.title: Single Sign-On
sso.subtitle: Let users from your product sign in without a second login
sso.ownersOnlyHint: Only workspace owners can manage SSO signing secrets. Ask an owner if you need access.
sso.secretsDesc: Shared JWT secrets your backend uses to sign SSO tokens.
sso.emptyHint: Create a secret, then use it on your backend to sign SSO tokens for your users.
sso.rotationMid: create a new one, switch your product to it, then
sso.rotationEnd: the old one (re-enable if something breaks). Delete it once you're sure. Tokens are verified against every enabled secret.
sso.deleteDescription: will be permanently deleted. Any tokens signed with it stop working immediately. This can't be undone — if you only want to pause it, disable it instead.
sso.renameDesc: Update the label used to tell this secret apart. The key itself is unchanged.
sso.createDesc: A new HS256 secret your backend uses to sign SSO tokens. You can reveal and copy it again any time.
sso.labelPlaceholder: e.g. Production, EU region, mobile app
sso.labelHint: Helps you tell multiple secrets apart when rotating.
sso.guideEndpointDesc: Redirect signed-in users from your product to this URL.
sso.guideParamReturnTo: Where to land after sign-in. Same-host path or URL only (host-whitelisted). Defaults to /.
sso.guideJwtPayloadDesc: The signed-in user's info, carried in the token. FeedLog reads only these fields — any other claims are ignored.
sso.guidePayloadExp: Expiry (Unix seconds). Required — see TTL below.
sso.guideSignDesc: Build the link on your backend.
sso.guideTtlBody: Recommended ~1 hour; hard cap 24 hours (±60s clock tolerance). Pre-sign tokens when you render the page — not when the user clicks — to avoid an extra round-trip.
sso.guideMatchedByPost: . A first-time email creates an end-user account (no extra login screen); changing a user's email creates a new account and does not migrate history.

workspace.title: Workspace
workspace.subtitle: Your workspace's identity
workspace.generalDesc: How your workspace appears to members and in invitation emails.
workspace.nameHint: Your team or company name. Shown to members and in invitation emails.

branding.title: Branding
branding.subtitle: How your public feedback portal looks to visitors.

portal.titleHint: Shown as the main heading on your feedback portal.
portal.descriptionPlaceholder: Any bug reports and feature requests are welcome...
portal.descriptionHint: Shown below the title to provide more context for visitors. Markdown supported.

members.title: Members
members.subtitle: Manage who can access this workspace
members.lastOwnerNote: The last owner of a workspace cannot be demoted or removed. Transfer ownership first.
members.inviteDesc: Send an email invitation. Invitations expire in 7 days.
members.emailNotConfiguredHint: No Resend API key set. The invitation will be created but no email is sent — copy the link from the list and share it manually.
members.ownerWarning: Owners have full administrative access — managing members, settings, and ownership. Only invite people you fully trust.
members.inviteToastEmailOn: If the email doesn't arrive, copy the link from the list to share it manually.
members.inviteToastEmailOff: No email was sent — copy the link from the list to share it manually.

contributor.label: Contributor
contributor.desc: View feedback from inside the dashboard and join discussions.

manager.label: Manager
manager.desc: Manage feedback, changelogs, and the roadmap.

owner.label: Owner
owner.desc: Full access. Can manage members, settings, and ownership.
